assertions.title: Backgrounding mid-onboarding preserves or safely resumes
assertions.reason: The onboarding edge-case suite passed both VAL-UX-048 tests: after a background/foreground cycle the route matrix lands on an allowed resume state (BiometricUnlock in the exercised path, or RecoveryPhrase if the phrase survives), and the test explicitly rejects Loading/Welcome/BiometricSetup/BiometricUnavailable dead-end states.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-048' -e 'VAL-UX-049' -e 'backgrounding mid-onboarding' -e 'resumeRoute' -e "\['RecoveryPhrase', 'BiometricUnlock'\]" -e 'initializeFirstLaunch resolves and populates recoveryPhrase' -e 'generateAndStoreSecret' -e 'not invoke global.fetch directly' src/features/auth/screens/__tests__/onboarding-edge-cases.test.tsx src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts src/features/session/get-initial-route.ts src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts
testNames: after initializeFirstLaunch + background/foreground cycle, navigator matrix routes to BiometricUnlock (never a blank/dead state)
if recoveryPhrase somehow survives the background cycle, the user is re-shown RecoveryPhrase (no stranded state)

assertions.title: Offline first-launch succeeds
assertions.reason: The same onboarding edge-case suite passed both offline-first tests: initializeFirstLaunch still resolved to a 24-word recovery phrase while a mocked global.fetch rejected with airplane-mode errors, the store ended with agent + recoveryPhrase populated and biometricState ready, and the suite verified generateAndStoreSecret ran once while initializeFirstLaunch itself never called fetch directly.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-048' -e 'VAL-UX-049' -e 'backgrounding mid-onboarding' -e 'resumeRoute' -e "\['RecoveryPhrase', 'BiometricUnlock'\]" -e 'initializeFirstLaunch resolves and populates recoveryPhrase' -e 'generateAndStoreSecret' -e 'not invoke global.fetch directly' src/features/auth/screens/__tests__/onboarding-edge-cases.test.tsx src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts src/features/session/get-initial-route.ts src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts
testNames: initializeFirstLaunch resolves and populates recoveryPhrase even when global.fetch rejects (airplane-mode simulation)
initializeFirstLaunch does not invoke global.fetch directly during the biometric sealing + HD derivation path

assertions.title: Wallet-connect scanner regression (no-regression)
assertions.reason: The wallet-connect scanner regression suite passed 10/10 and covered the required surface: requestCameraPermission runs on mount, the screen transitions from the loading placeholder to the camera view, the camera is wired for QR scanning, blocked permission exposes Open Settings, and scanned URLs are forwarded to walletConnectStore.handleIncomingUrl with navigation back on success.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-051' -e 'requestCameraPermission' -e 'transitions from the loading placeholder to <Camera>' -e 'allowedBarcodeTypes' -e 'handleIncomingUrl' -e 'Open Settings' -e 'scanBarcode' src/features/connect/screens/__tests__/wallet-connect-scanner-screen.test.tsx src/features/connect/screens/wallet-connect-scanner-screen.tsx
testNames: transitions from the loading placeholder to <Camera> once permission is granted
wires the Camera element with scanBarcode + allowedBarcodeTypes=[qr] and an onReadCode handler
shows an "Open Settings" deep link when permission is permanently blocked
forwards scanned URLs to walletConnectStore.handleIncomingUrl and navigates back

assertions.title: DID resolution / search regression (no-regression)
assertions.reason: The SearchScreen regression suite passed 7/7 and confirmed the biometric refactor did not change DID resolution: pressing Resolve with a DID calls agent.did.resolve(query.trim()), renders the resolved document on success, and preserves the no-PIN regression guard.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-052' -e 'agent.did.resolve' -e 'calls agent.did.resolve with the trimmed DID' -e 'Search DID' -e 'Resolve' src/features/search/screens/__tests__/search-screen.test.tsx src/features/search/screens/search-screen.tsx
testNames: calls agent.did.resolve with the trimmed DID and renders the document on success
renders the inline error card when the resolver reports an error
does not render any PIN-era copy (regression guard)

assertions.title: Settings non-reset rows regression
assertions.reason: The settings non-reset regression suite passed 9/9 and covered every required non-reset row: the tree contains no PIN/passcode copy, the Biometric unlock row remains a disabled indicator, the About section renders the package.json version string, Privacy policy and Terms of service press handlers call Linking.openURL with the exact URLs, and non-reset rows never trigger deleteSecret or reset side effects.

commands: rg -n -e 'VAL-UX-053' -e 'queryByText\(/\\bPIN\\b/i\)' -e 'Biometric unlock' -e 'App version' -e 'Privacy policy' -e 'Terms of service' -e 'Linking.openURL' src/features/settings/screens/__tests__/settings-non-reset-rows.test.tsx src/features/settings/screens/settings-screen.tsx
testNames: renders the Agent DID row without any PIN references
renders the "Biometric unlock" status indicator row as a disabled biometric reference (never PIN)
renders the About section with the package.json version string
renders the "Privacy policy" row and invokes Linking.openURL with the exact URL on press
renders the "Terms of service" row and invokes Linking.openURL with the exact URL on press

commandsRun.observation: PASS; the onboarding edge-case suite passed 7/7, including the assigned VAL-UX-048 and VAL-UX-049 cases.

summary: Tested 5 assigned onboarding edge/non-core regression assertions on the jest-local surface: 5 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Ran 4 targeted Jest suites (33 passing tests total) plus 4 supporting rg evidence scans, and saved all cited outputs under the edge-regressions evidence directory.
